message:
feat(abi): port mq_clocksend + mq_clockreceive (clockid_t variants of POSIX mq_timed*) (bd-rcrw7)

glibc 2.34 added clockid_t variants of mq_timedsend and
mq_timedreceive. The kernel mq_timed{send,receive} syscalls only
understand CLOCK_REALTIME, so for CLOCK_MONOTONIC we rebase the
caller's abstime onto realtime via:

    realtime_abs = realtime_now + (mono_abs - mono_now)

then forward to our existing mq_timed* implementations.

  - mq_clocksend(mqdes, msg, len, prio, clockid, abstime):
      CLOCK_REALTIME or NULL abstime -> direct mq_timedsend forward
      CLOCK_MONOTONIC                 -> rebase + mq_timedsend
      anything else                   -> errno=EINVAL, return -1
  - mq_clockreceive(mqdes, msg, len, *prio, clockid, abstime):
      same dispatch rules; isize return matches mq_timedreceive.

Tests: 4 abi tests (CLOCK_REALTIME round-trip, CLOCK_MONOTONIC
round-trip, invalid clockid -> EINVAL, queue-full timeout -> ETIMEDOUT
through both clocksend and clockreceive paths).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

-{"id":"bd-cmimx","title":"PORT: ns_sprintrr + ns_sprintrrf (libresolv RR formatter, common types + RFC3597 fallback)","description":"libresolv ns_sprintrr(handle, rr, name_ctx, origin, buf, buflen) is a wrapper that reads fields from a CNsRr/CNsMsg pair and forwards to ns_sprintrrf. ns_sprintrrf(msg, msglen, name, class, type, ttl, rdata, rdlen, name_ctx, origin, buf, buflen) formats one DNS resource record in BIND zone-file syntax: "name TTL class type rdata". TTL via ns_format_ttl. Class: 1=IN, 2=CS, 3=CH, 4=HS, else "CLASSn". Type: A=1, NS=2, CNAME=5, PTR=12, MX=15, AAAA=28, TXT=16; else "TYPEn". Per-type rdata: A=dotted quad, AAAA=inet_ntop(AF_INET6), NS/CNAME/PTR=dn_expand from rdata, MX="pref name", TXT="\"string\" \"string\"" with backslash-escaping. Unknown types use RFC 3597 generic notation: "\\# rdlen hexbytes". Returns chars written or -1 on overflow / parse error. Both handle NULL buf via -1.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-26T16:26:25.156683691Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T16:26:25.156683691Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

TTL via ns_format_ttl. Class: 1=IN, 2=CS, 3=CH, 4=HS, else "CLASSn". Type: A=1, NS=2, CNAME=5, PTR=12, MX=15, AAAA=28, TXT=16; else "TYPEn". Per-type rdata: A=dotted quad, AAAA=inet_ntop(AF_INET6), NS/CNAME/PTR=dn_expand from rdata, MX="pref name", TXT="\"string\" \"string\"" with backslash-escaping. Unknown types use RFC 3597 generic notation: "\\# rdlen hexbytes". Returns chars written or -1 on overflow / parse error. Both handle NULL buf via -1.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-26T16:26:25.156683691Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T16:33:05.165407951Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T16:33:05.163322872Z","close_reason":"shipped: ns_sprintrr + ns_sprintrrf with per-type rdata formatters for A/AAAA/NS/CNAME/PTR/MX/TXT and RFC 3597 generic fallback for unknown types + 8 abi tests including end-to-end through ns_initparse/ns_parserr","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-cmiv","title":"EPIC: Implement real DNS resolution for getaddrinfo","description":"getaddrinfo() currently only resolves:
1. Numeric IP addresses (127.0.0.1, ::1)
2. Hostnames found in /etc/hosts

It does NOT perform actual DNS queries. This means programs like wget, curl (for non-localhost URLs), dig, nslookup, and any network client that resolves hostnames will fail.

OPTIONS:
A) Delegate to host libc's getaddrinfo for non-numeric, non-/etc/hosts hostnames
B) Implement a minimal DNS stub resolver (send UDP query to nameserver from /etc/resolv.conf)
C) Both: try our resolver first, fall back to host

Option A is fastest to implement and most reliable. Option B gives us full independence from host libc.

ALREADY DONE: /etc/hosts lookup works, numeric IP parsing works, freeaddrinfo correctly frees our Box-allocated structures.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:07:15.603558212Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-03T18:43:31.403160236Z","closed_at":"2026-04-03T18:43:31.402959135Z","close_reason":"Already implemented. getaddrinfo has a full native DNS stub resolver: udp_dns_query() sends A/AAAA queries to /etc/resolv.conf nameservers via SYS_socket/SYS_sendto/SYS_recvfrom with configurable timeout/retry. ResolverConfig parses nameservers, search domains, ndots. Resolution chain: numeric -> /etc/hosts -> DNS -> repair fallback. 46 resolv tests pass. Previously verified when closing bd-zh1y.7 and bd-1y7.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-cmiv.1","title":"Add host libc delegation for DNS-requiring getaddrinfo calls","description":"When getaddrinfo receives a hostname that:
1. Is NOT a numeric IP address
2. Is NOT found in /etc/hosts

Delegate to the host libc's getaddrinfo via our ELF resolver. The host's getaddrinfo will perform the actual DNS query.

IMPLEMENTATION:
1. resolve_host_symbol_raw("getaddrinfo") to get host's function pointer
2. Call host_getaddrinfo(node, service, hints, res) 
3. Return the host's result directly (the addrinfo structs are heap-allocated by the host)
4. In freeaddrinfo, detect whether the addrinfo was allocated by us (Box) or host (malloc) and free accordingly

TRICKY PART: freeaddrinfo must distinguish our Box-allocated addrinfos from host-malloc-allocated ones. Could use a sentinel field or check if the pointer is in our Box allocation range.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:07:15.811880807Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:30:57.450087855Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T03:30:57.448613314Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-cmiv.1","depends_on_id":"bd-cmiv","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:07:15.811880807Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-cnf1","title":"CONFORMANCE: libc sysconf+pathconf+confstr diff matrix","description":"Differential harness shipped (see commit). 4 functions: sysconf (12 common names cross-checked), pathconf (/, _PC_NAME_MAX/_PC_PATH_MAX), fpathconf (/dev/null), confstr (_CS_PATH size+content, _CS_GNU_LIBC_VERSION zero-match). Discovered DISC-SYSCONF-001: fl returns POSIX min for _SC_CHILD_MAX/_SC_STREAM_MAX while glibc queries live rlimit. Discovered DISC-SYSCONF-002: fl returns POSIX min for _PC_LINK_MAX (127) while glibc queries actual filesystem (65000 on ext4). Both documented; bd-cnf2 opened. 8 tests, 0 substantive divergences.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"cod-frankenlibc","created_at":"2026-04-24T15:30:00Z","created_by":"cod-frankenlibc","updated_at":"2026-04-24T15:30:00Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T15:30:00Z","close_reason":"Harness shipped; documented divergences logged.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","differential","sysconf"]}
@@ -1071,6 +1071,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-r25ks","title":"REVIEW: [HIGH] abort() does not unblock SIGABRT before raising — POSIX violation, suppresses core dump","description":"In crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/stdlib_abi.rs at lines 2297-2309, abort() raises SIGABRT via crate::signal_abi::raise without first unblocking SIGABRT.

POSIX 2008 mandates: 'If the SIGABRT signal is being held or ignored, abort() shall first unblock or unignore the SIGABRT signal.'

Current implementation:
1. fflush stdio
2. raise(SIGABRT)  -- if blocked, signal stays pending, no delivery, no termination
3. signal(SIGABRT, SIG_DFL)
4. raise(SIGABRT)  -- still blocked, still no delivery
5. sys_exit_group(134)  -- fallback, but no core dump

Consequences:
- Process exits with status 134, but no core file is generated (production debugging is impaired)
- POSIX-compliant programs that block SIGABRT around critical sections expect abort() to override the block; they will instead silently continue execution (because raise returns without delivery) -- but here we have sys_exit_group as fallback so termination is preserved
- The user's SIG_DFL re-raise behavior is bypassed when SIGABRT is blocked

Fix: Before the first raise(), call sigprocmask(SIG_UNBLOCK, ...) with a sigset containing only SIGABRT. Match glibc abort.c behavior: also clear SIG_IGN by resetting to SIG_DFL if currently SIG_IGN.

Severity: HIGH -- POSIX non-compliance + lost coredumps in production crashes.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-24T01:54:15.710736731Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-24T02:36:11.148658121Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T02:36:11.146362986Z","close_reason":"abort() unblocks SIGABRT before raise; POSIX-compliant; commit 64fb99b2","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-r2oq","title":"Add search.h conformance fixtures and harness coverage","description":"Add dedicated tests/conformance/fixtures/search_ops.json and frankenlibc-harness coverage for <search.h> ABI surface (hcreate/hsearch/hdestroy, tsearch/tfind/tdelete/twalk, lfind/lsearch, insque/remque), following existing conformance fixture patterns and validating schema/spec coverage.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-16T21:47:12.767248867Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T03:49:35.833569558Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T03:49:35.831673850Z","close_reason":"Added explicit search.h fixture coverage and fixed host-parity ABI behavior for hsearch/remque","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-r3qc","title":"syscall: pidfd_send_signal invalid flags should not override invalid pidfd","description":"Host glibc returns EBADF for pidfd_send_signal(-1, 0, NULL, 1), so invalid pidfd still dominates bad flags. Add ABI regression coverage to freeze that precedence contract exactly.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"AmberSignal","created_at":"2026-04-21T07:57:01.993657584Z","created_by":"AmberSignal","updated_at":"2026-04-21T07:58:57.900227304Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T07:58:57.897016719Z","close_reason":"Locked down host-parity pidfd_send_signal(-1, 0, NULL, 1) behavior: invalid flags do not override invalid pidfd, so the veneer still reports exact EBADF; verified locally and via rch.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"bd-rcrw7","title":"PORT: mq_clocksend + mq_clockreceive (clockid_t variants of POSIX mq_timed*)","description":"POSIX mq_clocksend(mqdes, msg, len, prio, clockid, abstime) and mq_clockreceive(mqdes, msg, len, *prio, clockid, abstime) — clockid_t variants of mq_timedsend/receive added in glibc 2.34. Dispatch on clockid: CLOCK_REALTIME forwards directly to mq_timedsend/mq_timedreceive (which already use the realtime kernel ABI). CLOCK_MONOTONIC converts the supplied monotonic abstime into a realtime abstime via "realtime_abs = realtime_now + (mono_abs - mono_now)" then forwards. Other clockids return -1 with errno=EINVAL. NULL abstime forwards as-is (means "block forever" per POSIX).","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-26T16:37:11.468866720Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T16:37:11.468866720Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-rcw4p","title":"syscall: pidfd_getfd self pidfd with invalid targetfd should return EBADF exactly","description":"Host glibc returns EBADF for pidfd_getfd(self_pidfd, -1, 0). Add focused ABI regression coverage for the invalid-targetfd contract on a valid pidfd.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T09:52:51.342051427Z","created_by":"AmberSignal","updated_at":"2026-04-22T04:54:23.342856887Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T04:54:23.339853631Z","close_reason":"pidfd_getfd(self, -1, 0) → EBADF pinned.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-rdxo4","title":"PORT: __cxa_vec_new/new2/new3 + __cxa_vec_delete/delete2/delete3 + __cxa_vec_cctor","description":"Add the Itanium C++ ABI vector new/delete/copy-ctor family (7 entries) on top of our existing __cxa_vec_ctor/dtor primitives. (1) __cxa_vec_new(count, size, padding, ctor, dtor) -> *mut c_void: malloc(count*size + padding), if padding > 0 stash count at the start of the padding so __cxa_vec_delete can recover it, run ctor on each element, return array (= raw + padding). (2)/(3) __cxa_vec_new2/3 with custom alloc/dealloc functions. (4) __cxa_vec_delete(array, size, padding, dtor): if array NULL no-op; recover raw + count from padding, run dtor reverse, free raw. (5)/(6) __cxa_vec_delete2/3 with custom dealloc (delete2 takes ptr-only, delete3 takes ptr+size). (7) __cxa_vec_cctor(dest, src, count, size, ctor, dtor): copy-constructor variant. NULL ctor/dtor must be tolerated. count*size+padding uses checked arithmetic; overflow returns NULL. Implementations route through our crate::malloc_abi::malloc/free except where the user supplied custom allocators.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-26T15:15:56.551953229Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T15:20:51.929936674Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T15:20:51.927873908Z","close_reason":"shipped: 7 cxa vector new/delete/cctor primitives w/ count-in-padding stashing + 8 abi tests including round-trip and custom alloc/dealloc, all green","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-reax","title":"EPIC: Replace critical stubs with real implementations or host delegation","description":"The comprehensive audit identified several categories of stub/placeholder implementations:

1. SAFE NO-OP STUBS (~50 functions): Return 0/-1/NULL without doing real work. These are intentional for features we don't support (profiling, NSS, netgroups). LOW priority — they don't crash, just silently no-op.

2. HOST CALL-THROUGH DEBT (~70 symbols): Functions that delegate to host glibc via RTLD_NEXT. These work correctly but represent symbols we haven't implemented natively yet. LOW priority — they work fine via delegation.

3. CORE TERMIOS STUBS: tcgetattr/tcsetattr/tcflush/tcflow in frankenlibc-core return -1. NOT a real issue because the ABI layer (termios_abi.rs) uses raw ioctl syscalls directly and never calls these core stubs.

4. RPC SUBSYSTEM (~68 stubs): Return safe defaults. MEDIUM priority — RPC is legacy but some programs still use it.

5. ELF LOADER INCOMPLETE: Only Phase-1 relocations; IFUNC deferred. MEDIUM priority — affects shared library loading.

RATIONALE: Focus on items that break real programs first. The setjmp/longjmp stubs were already fixed (now delegate to host). The on_exit handler gap was already fixed.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"PlumCove","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:04:55.983549992Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-02T19:47:38.984372338Z","closed_at":"2026-04-02T19:47:38.983957473Z","close_reason":"Both children closed: bd-reax.1 (RPC host delegation) and bd-reax.2 (IFUNC relocation). Remaining categories: safe no-op stubs (intentional for unsupported features like profiling/NSS/netgroups), host call-through (working via delegation), and core termios stubs (ABI layer uses raw ioctl, never calls core stubs). No critical stubs remain.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
